title: Billie Eilisch, Finneas O'Connell Win Song of the Year, Slam ICE at Grammys
text:
Billie Eilisch's 2026 Grammy Win and ICE Statement

Song of the Year Achievement: Billie Eilsch and her brother Finneas O'Connell won the Grammy Award for Song of the Year for "Wildflower" at the 2026 Grammys held February 1 at Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles. This marks their third victory in this category—Eilsch previously won for "bad guy" and the "Barbie" soundtrack contribution.

ICE Protest Statement: During her acceptance speech, Eilsch made a political statement: "no one is illegal on stolen land." She elaborated by saying "Our voices really do matter and the people matter" and encouraged audiences to "keep fighting and speaking up and protesting." She concluded with profanity directed at ICE that was censored during the CBS broadcast.

Additional Context: Eilsch wore an "ICE OUT" pin on the red carpet, visibly signaling her stance on immigration enforcement. She competed against nominees including Lady Gaga, Kendrick Lamar, Bad Bunny, and others in this category.
